1.title: Shutter Speed
1.description: Shutter speed dictates how long the camera sensor is exposed to light. Fast speeds freeze action, while slow speeds blur motion.

2.title: Aperture
2.description: Aperture controls the size of the lens opening. A larger aperture (smaller f-number) captures a blurry background (shallow depth of field).

3.title: ISO
3.description: ISO measures the sensor's sensitivity to light. A higher ISO brightens the image in dark settings, but introduces visual noise or 'grain'.

1.question: Apply the settings to freeze the action, blur the background, and keep the image clean.

2.question: Capture heavy motion blur, keep the background sharp, and keep the image clean.

3.question: Isolate the subject with a shallow depth of field, keeping it sharp and clean.

4.question: Brighten this dark alleyway to see details, accepting the heavy film grain.
